title: MATRIYA Capability Plan
description: STABLE v1.0. The output artifact of the Capability Planner — the PURE planning layer that connects Scientific Task Contract -> Capability Ontology -> Engine Contract -> Decision Workspace. It is a resolver, NOT a Composer and NOT an executor: it translates a task's capability graph into an engine plan and the emissions that will feed a Decision Workspace. It NAMES NO ENGINE INSIDE THE TASK (the task stays capability-only; the plan is where engines first appear), it EXECUTES NOTHING (no results), and — the Decision Boundary — it may assemble Decision Workspace inputs but may never emit or select a decision. If a required capability is unmet, it REPORTS A TYPED GAP instead of inventing a plan. Documentation only.

referencesEngines.description: Echo of the task contract: the TASK names no engine. Engines appear only in this plan's steps.

status.description: 'planned' = every capability node covered; 'partial' = some non-critical nodes gap; 'blocked' = a required node unmet. Gaps are reported, never papered over.

edges.description: Plan DAG — mirrors the task capabilityGraph edges.

workspacePlan.description: How the planned emissions map to Decision Workspace sections. The plan TERMINATES at the workspace — never at a decision.

executed.description: A plan is never executed. It carries no results — only intended engine invocations and their declared emission types.

selectionCriteria.description: The ordered rules used to pick engines. Informational; documents how selection was made.

allOf.$comment: An uncovered step MUST carry a typed gap — never a silent hole.

emit.description: Emission kinds from Engine Contract v1.1. 'decision' is deliberately absent — the planner can never plan a decision.

nodeId.description: Matches the task capabilityGraph node id.

capability.description: Ontology id (primitive or derived) from the task node.

requiredPrimitives.description: Derived capabilities expanded to primitives via the Capability Ontology.

assignments.description: One assignment per required primitive: the engine chosen to satisfy it and what it will emit.

engine.description: The engine name (engines appear here — never in the task).

reason.description: WHY the capability is unmet — a typed reason (sibling of the Constraint Resolution Engine's 'which constraint blocks'). Never 'no solution' with no cause.
